Ad Text:
$71.54-80.35ph + super
Brisbane CBD Location with WFH Flexibility provided
12 month temp contract with potential for extension

About the Role

Our client are a Brisbane based local government entity based in the Brisbane CBD. They are currently seeking a Principal Process Analyst to come in and join their team on a 12 month temp contract with potential for extension.

Candidates for this role must have:

Comprehensive experience in finance related activities including the end-to-end finance processes.
Demonstrated experience in Process mapping and documentation leveraging Process Management Best Practice
Demonstrated experience to provide sound finance process and systems advice, managing highly sensitive and confidential information.
Experience with process improvement methodologies (e.g., Lean, Six Sigma).
Previous State or local government experience (Desirable)

Key responsibilities will include:

Customer-Centric Design: Lead the creation of finance processes and procedures that prioritize user experience and service excellence.
Strategic Collaboration: Build strong partnerships across Council divisions to align finance systems with broader organizational goals.
Process Documentation: Elicit and document finance workflows (Levels 1-4) using BPMN standards within corporate systems.
Business Transformation: Conduct AS-IS and TO-BE analysis to support the transition to new ways of working during transformation projects.
Performance Management: Develop and monitor KPIs for finance processes to ensure they meet Council's strategic and service objectives.
Knowledge & Training: Coordinate knowledge management and develop training materials to ensure staff proficiency in finance systems.
Continuous Improvement & QA: Lead process improvement initiatives and conduct quality assurance reviews to ensure compliance and internal control.

About You

You will be an experienced Process Analyst with proven experience and reliability in previous positions.

Comprehensive experience in finance related activities including the end-to-end finance processes.
Demonstrated experience in Process mapping and documentation leveraging Process Management Best Practice
Demonstrated experience to provide sound finance process and systems advice, managing highly sensitive and confidential information.
Experience with process improvement methodologies (e.g., Lean, Six Sigma).
Previous State or local government experience (Desirable)

At Randstad, we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity to the benefit of all. We actively encourage applications from any background.

Organisation Profile
Our client is a leading FMCG multinational operating a vertically integrated model from manufacturing through to major retail customers such as Coles and Woolworths, the organisation is recognised for its market-leading brands, operational scale, and ongoing investment in growth and innovation.
The finance team plays a highly strategic role in shaping performance and commercial decision making, partnering closely with senior leaders across the business.

Role Overview
This is a highly visible commercial finance role partnering directly with ELT, Directors and senior finance leaders to drive performance, strategic investment decisions, and long-term profitability.
The role sits at the centre of commercial decision making and offers exposure to major initiatives including long-range financial modelling, strategy development, and evaluation of large-scale capital investments.

Key Responsibilities
Partner with Directors to manage P&L performance and influence strategic commercial priorities
Lead long-range financial modelling, scenario analysis, and ambition planning to support strategy
Evaluate major capital investment initiatives ($100m+), including business case development and post-investment reviews
Provide financial insight and challenge across key commercial initiatives, including large customer negotiations
Translate complex financial and operational data into clear insights and recommendations for senior leadership
Collaborate across Supply Chain, Operations, Marketing, Commercial Finance, and Revenue Growth teams
Prepare executive-level presentations to support decision making across senior leadership and APAC stakeholders
Drive continuous improvement across financial modelling, reporting, and decision support capability
Ideal Candidate
You are a highly analytical and commercially minded finance professional who enjoys partnering closely with senior stakeholders to influence strategic decisions.
You bring strong financial modelling capability and the ability to structure complex problems, translate data into clear insights, and communicate recommendations with confidence and clarity.
This role will suit candidates coming from commercial finance, strategic finance, corporate finance, or consulting backgrounds who are motivated to work in a high-impact, commercially focused environment.

Key Requirements
CA qualified (or equivalent)
Approximately 6–10 years’ experience in commercial finance, FP&A, strategic finance, corporate finance, or consulting
Strong financial modelling capability including scenario modelling, long-range planning, and business case development
Proven ability to partner with senior stakeholders and influence commercial decision making
Advanced Excel skills with exposure to Power BI or similar tools highly regarded
Experience within FMCG, consumer goods, retail, manufacturing, or large corporate environments advantageous
Big 4 background (Consulting, Deals, or Advisory) highly regarded

Senior Technical Business Analyst – 12 Month FTC

Location: Sydney | Hybrid Working

An exciting opportunity has opened for an experienced Senior Technical Business Analyst to join a leading Australian organisation operating at scale across multiple locations nationwide.

This role will play a key part in delivering strategic technology initiatives and improving digital capability across the business. You will support a variety of programs ranging from large transformation projects to smaller business improvement initiatives.

You’ll act as the bridge between business stakeholders and technology teams, helping translate complex requirements into clear, structured technical artefacts that enable effective solution delivery.

What You’ll Be Doing

Leading requirements gathering and translating business needs into clear technical documentation including functional specifications, user stories, process flows, and data mappings

Facilitating workshops and engaging with stakeholders across multiple business units to identify opportunities for improvement

Supporting the delivery lifecycle end-to-end including analysis, solution design support, testing preparation, risk management, and issue resolution

Conducting process analysis and mapping (AS-IS / TO-BE) to improve operational efficiency and customer outcomes

Contributing to technology initiatives such as CRM platform enhancements, data governance improvements, and broader digital transformation projects

Working closely with technology teams to ensure solutions are aligned with architecture, integration patterns, and delivery standards

What We’re Looking For

Extensive experience working as a Technical Business Analyst in complex environments

Strong experience producing structured technical documentation including FSDs, user stories, and process models

Solid understanding of software delivery lifecycle (SDLC) and data governance principles

Proven ability to work with both technical teams and non-technical stakeholders, simplifying complex concept

Strong facilitation, stakeholder management, and problem-solving skills




This is a great opportunity for a hands-on Technical BA who enjoys working across a broad range of initiatives and partnering closely with both business and technology teams to deliver meaningful outcomes.




Due to the high volume of applications, please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

About Ticketek Entertainment Group

Ticketek Entertainment Group is a global fan experience company that tickets, promotes and delivers live experiences that are impossible to forget.  In a distracted world where nothing beats real human moments, We make life better live!

Our Group includes; our Fan Experience Platform (Ticketek) that sells tickets and provides value added services, Event promoting, with businesses across Touring (TEG Touring), Sport (TEG Sport), and Family Experiences (TEG Experiences) and our digital business (Ovation) which focuses on delivering seamless data-driven outcomes for our fans and partner

About the Role

As a Lead Insight Analyst, you will be the technical lead for TEG’s analytics output, ensuring our data storytelling meets a state-of-the-art standard of accuracy and impact. In this hands-on role, you will oversee end-to-end workstreams, transforming complex data exploration into executive-ready strategic narratives.

Working with Data & Analytics leadership, you will provide the SQL mastery and technical mentorship required to elevate the team’s craft without direct people management. Your goal is to institutionalize best practices in reporting and visualization, providing the rigorous insights necessary to drive a data-driven culture across our global live entertainment business.



Requirements



What a day in the life looks like?

Insight Delivery Oversight: oversee the end-to-end analytics workstreams to ensure that insights are accurate, timely, and aligned with strategic business priorities.
Technical Mentoring: Serve as the technical lead and mentor, providing guidance on SQL, visualization, and advanced methodologies without the responsibility of direct people management.
Hands-on Analysis: Perform deep-dive analysis on complex customer, commercial, and digital datasets to solve high-priority business challenges.
Strategic Storytelling: Develop compelling narratives and executive-ready dashboards that translate multi-channel data into actionable strategies for senior leadership.
Cross-functional Collaboration: Partner with Data Science, Engineering, and Product teams to ensure data integrity and provide insights for tactical decision-making.
Best Practice Leadership: Define and promote standards for data visualization and reporting to ensure consistent, professional output across the insights function.

About you

Essential experience & skills:

Minimum of 7 years experience in a data-heavy environment, ideally within entertainment, e-commerce, or retail.
A tertiary degree in a quantitative field (e.g. Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, or Data Science).
Proven record of going beyond simple reporting to deliver strategic insights that drive commercial growth.
Possess a strong understanding of the wider data ecosystem, including data science and ML methodologies.
Have expert-level knowledge of SQL and use of tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker to create high-performance, intuitive dashboards.
Experience using hands-on guidance and standard-setting to elevate a team's technical abilities.
The ability to transform complex data into clear stories that focus on "the why" behind the numbers and turn vague business questions into structured, actionable analytical projects.


Benefits



Here’s a taste of what TEG offers: 

Complimentary event tickets
Birthday and volunteering leave
Wellbeing discounts & flu vaccinations
Paid parental leave & free employee support (EAP)
Global rewards and recognition
Learning, development & career pathways
A diverse, inclusive, and passionate team

Equal opportunities

TEG is an equal opportunity employer committed to embrace diversity, respect, and care for our people and communities. 

If there are any adjustments that need to be made to ensure you have a fair and equitable experience in our recruitment process, please advise us when scheduling your interview.

Norton Rose Fulbright is a global legal practice. We provide the world's pre-eminent corporations and financial institutions with a full business law service. We have more than 4000 lawyers based in over 58 offices across Europe, the United States, Canada, Latin America, Asia, Australia, Africa, the Middle East and Central Asia.

We are currently looking for a Senior Finance Business Analyst to join our high performing finance team, based in Sydney. Reporting to the Financial Planning and Analysis Manager you will provide high quality and proactive commercial advice and financial analysis including financial support and insights, value add solutions, financial and management reporting to partners, lawyers and business services staff to enable Norton Rose Fulbright to deliver seamless client service and ensure the smooth operation of our legal practice teams. 

Responsibilities:

Develop a strong understanding of the business imperatives and key practice drivers that drive profitability of the firm.
Financial reporting and analysis, including the preparation and review of monthly management reports and the communication of results to relevant stakeholders
Prepare and provide financial insights into performance reporting for fee earners and legal teams (i.e. utilisation, realisation recovery, profitability and financial performance reviews)
Prepare financial information and reports including profitability and expense reporting for legal and business support teams as well as commentary on expense variances for monthly financial reporting
Business partnering with senior stakeholders across legal and business services groups in relation to key performance drivers, firm budgets, actuals vs variance analysis and forecasts to drive good financial hygiene practices.
Contribute to driving good financial governance in the Firm, and providing a financial lens on general business activities such as recruitment, client secondments and expenditure.
Collaboration with finance senior leadership, Legal teams and Business Services Directors and Managers to prepare timely and accurate budgets and forecasts that meet both local and global requirements.
Maintenance of revenue, expense and KPI budgets in the relevant systems.
Commitment to the development of a uniform and standard form of data presentation in response to requests, including assisting with the ongoing development of financial dashboards.

About You:

You will aspire to be a true business partner, demonstrating strong commercial acumen and integrity, and the ability to build trusting relationships with internal clients through exceptional stakeholder management skills. You will have a keen interest in telling the “story behind the numbers” as well as being a confident communicator who can tailor your communication style to stakeholders with and without a financial background. Candidates should demonstrate:

Client service-orientated focus with the ability to professionally communicate with internal clients at all levels.
Demonstrated commercial awareness, with the ability to understand how key business and practice drivers impact financial performance. Prior experience in a professional services environment not essential but will be very highly regarded.
CA or CPA qualified
Strong written and verbal communication skills including a proven ability to draft non-routine correspondence and documentation.
Highly effective team player who is positive, communicative and proactive, and willing to work closely with other teams in Finance (Financial Accounting and Finance Systems)
Highly organised with a proactive approach to problem solving.
Ability to work under pressure to meet tight deadlines and balance competing priorities.
Excellent attention to detail and professional presentation.
Strong user of Microsoft Excel and previous exposure to Power BI.

About the Role:

We are seeking a dynamic and highly experienced SuccessFactors Employee Central Lead to oversee and drive EC related projects, continuous enhancements, and innovation initiatives. This role goes beyond operational support, focusing on delivering strategic improvements, leading functional design, and shaping the direction of EC capabilities in partnership with HR and IT leadership.
You will champion EC best practices, guide configuration excellence, oversee integrations, and ensure the solution evolves to meet business needs. The ideal candidate brings strong functional depth, leadership capabilities, and a passion for driving digital HR transformation.

Key Responsibilities:

1. Leadership of EC Enhancements & Projects
•    Lead end to end delivery of EC enhancement initiatives, small to medium projects, and optimisation programs.
•    Own the EC roadmap and ensure alignment with organisational HR technology strategy.
•    Drive innovation by identifying new features, functionalities, and process improvements.
•    Act as the EC solution architect during project cycles (requirements, design, build, test, deploy).

2. Stakeholder & Business Engagement
•    Serve as the primary onshore EC functional lead and trusted advisor to HR stakeholders.
•    Facilitate workshops, gather requirements, define solution approaches, and present design proposals.
•    Engage and influence HR, IT, and leadership teams to ensure adoption of EC capabilities.
•    Translate complex technical concepts into clear business language.

3. Functional Design & Configuration Ownership
•    Lead configuration activities across Employee Central: 

Foundation Objects
Business Rules
Workflows
Data Models
Event Reason Derivation

•    Ensure consistent solution governance and best practice configuration standards.
•    Oversee regression testing, release readiness, and impact assessment for biannual SF releases.

4. Integration Leadership & Issue Resolution
•    Provide functional oversight for integrations between EC and: 

Other SuccessFactors modules (Recruiting, Onboarding, Compensation, PMGM, Time Off, etc.)
S/4HANA, SAP HCM, Payroll
CPI, Integration Center, APIs

•    Lead cross functional troubleshooting with offshore teams and technical experts.
•    Ensure robust integration designs that support scalable HR processes.

5. Data Governance & Quality Management
•    Oversee master data quality, validation processes, and governance controls.
•    Lead data migrations, mass updates, and template-based data loads.
•    Monitor EC data integrity and ensure compliance with HR policies.

6. Documentation, Governance & Continuous Improvement
•    Maintain high quality documentation, functional specs, SOPs, and design artefacts.
•    Ensure adherence to change management practices and audit controls.
•    Track KPIs for enhancement delivery and continuous improvement outcomes.
•    Mentor junior consultants and offshore team members.

Required Skills & Experience:

Technical & Functional Expertise
•    8–10+ years of end-to-end experience in SuccessFactors Employee Central (implementation or project-focused).
•    Deep knowledge of EC configuration, data models, business rules, and workflows.
•    Proven ability to lead EC projects, enhancements, and design discussions.
•    Extensive experience across integration touchpoints with S/4HANA or SAP ECC (PA/OM/Payroll).
•    Hands-on with CPI or Integration Center troubleshooting.
•    SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central certification (mandatory).

Leadership & Soft Skills
•    Strong stakeholder and project leadership capabilities.
•    Excellent communication and solution presentation skills.
•    Strategic thinker with strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
•    Comfortable leading in an onsite, business-facing environment.
•    Ability to collaborate effectively with global and offshore teams.

Nice to Have
•    Experience with HR processes in the Australian Energy or Resources sector.
•    Understanding of EC Payroll or SAP Payroll integrations.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer (12 month contract) - WildRift Data
Ad Text:
Riot's Data Engineers build products that improve the experience of our players worldwide using petabytes of data and state-of-the-art processing technology. Maximizing the potential of this data is a complex task. 

As a Senior Data Engineer (Contract) joining the Sydney-based team, you will lead the modernization and uplift of our data platform to improve reliability, governance, and scalability. You will drive architectural refactoring across our lakehouse environment, consolidate and standardize data models, and enforce a clear Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion hierarchy. You will implement robust data quality and observability frameworks for business-critical datasets, operationalize data ownership and tiering standards, and elevate metadata completeness and lineage transparency across our data catalog and warehouse ecosystem. Working closely with cross-functional stakeholders, you will help transform the data environment into a trusted, production-grade platform that supports operational decision-making, executive reporting, and advanced analytics. 

This role is based in Sydney and a 12-month contract.

Responsibilities:

Partner with senior engineers to execute architectural improvements across the lakehouse platform.
Implement dimensional data models and standardize semantic/gold reporting layers.
Enforce medallion (Bronze/Silver/Gold) architecture standards.
Build and integrate automated data quality monitoring.
Improve metadata completeness and lineage transparency through catalog updates and automation.
Contribute to CI/CD standards and pipeline reliability improvements.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to deliver production-grade, trusted datasets.
Required Qualifications: 

5+ years of relevant experience building scalable data platforms in production environments.
Strong experience in data modeling and data warehousing, e.g. medallion lakehouse architecture.
Strong hands-on expertise with Databricks, Spark (PySpark/SparkSQL), Delta Lake, Airflow, and Python.
Experience implementing automated data quality checks and validation frameworks.
Experience with dbt or similar transformation frameworks.
Experience delivering on AWS-based data platforms.
Familiarity with metadata/catalog tools (Atlan, Alation, DataHub, Unity Catalog, etc.).
Desired Qualifications:

Fluency in Mandarin Chinese is highly desirable.
Experience contributing to data platform modernization or data maturity uplift initiatives.
Experience working across cross-regional engineering teams.
Experience improving metadata automation or lineage tooling.

For this role, you'll find success through craft expertise, a collaborative spirit, and decision-making that prioritizes the delight of players. We will be looking at your past studies, experience, and your personal relationship with games. If you embody player empathy and care about players' experiences, this could be your role!

Riot Games fosters a player and workplace experience that values teamwork embodied by the Summoner's Code and Community Code. Our culture embraces differences as a strength, and our values are the guiding principles for how we approach work. We are committed to putting diversity and inclusion (D&I) at the center of everything we do, and promoting a fair and collaborative culture where Rioters treat one another with dignity and respect. We encourage you to read more about our value of thriving together and our ongoing work to build the most inclusive company in Gaming.

We are seeking a highly capable Project & Change Manager to lead and deliver strategic transformation relating to the integration of our EMEA and Australian Finance Functions. This hybrid role combines 75% project management responsibilities with 25% change management & adoption, ensuring that projects are delivered effectively while new ways of working are successfully embedded. Based in Sydney, this role operates within a complex, global environment and will work across multi-disciplinary teams to drive business value, optimise processes, and ensure change is understood, adopted, and sustained.

Key Responsibilities

Project Management (75%)

Act as the key contact between Sydney and London which may involve alternative time zone working.
Develop and maintain project documentation: project plans, RAID logs, action logs, risk assessments, status reporting, benefits tracking.
Analyse business processes and systems to identify opportunities for efficiency, standardisation, and optimisation.
Provide structure and guidance on ways of working, organisational design, and operational improvements.
Facilitate requirements sessions, and cross-functional discussions.
Identify and manage interdependencies across projects and business units.
Track and report project performance, KPIs, and benefits realisation.
Support colleagues by educating them on project delivery tools and frameworks.
Act decisively, operate with pace, and navigate ambiguity in a changeable environment.

Change Management & Adoption (25%)

Design and implement change and adoption approaches that align to programme needs and ensure successful adoption of new processes, tools, or technology.
Conduct stakeholder mapping, change impact assessments, and organisational readiness analyses.
Develop integrated communication and engagement plans using a range of media including written, graphic, digital, and face-to-face channels.
Develop and deliver training strategies to drive awareness, understanding, and adoption.
Define and track change success metrics, business value, ROI, and benefits realisation.
Partner with senior stakeholders to understand needs, expectations, and alignment to broader strategy.
Support horizon scanning, market insights, and analysis of impacts to the business model and workforce.
Contribute to the prioritisation of transformation opportunities across business functions.

Key Skills & Experience

Essential

Proven experience delivering complex, multi-geography transformation projects, ideally within professional services or similar industries.
Strong understanding of project management methodologies (e.g., PRINCE2, Agile, Waterfall, hybrid approaches).
Demonstrated experience in change management principles, frameworks, and communication strategies.
Excellent verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills, including the ability to deliver clear, persuasive presentations.
Strong analytical and problemâ€‘solving skills, including process analysis and modelling.
Experience managing senior stakeholders and influencing at all levels of an organisation.
High attention to detail, strong organisational skills, and the ability to prioritise within a fast-paced environment.
Flexible, adaptable, and comfortable working in ambiguous contexts.
Highly motivated, collaborative, and pragmatic self-starter.

Desirable

Understanding of digital communications and innovative engagement methods.
Exposure to organisational design activities.
Professional qualifications such as PRINCE2, APMP, Scrum, or Six Sigma.

Bank First was born by making a BOLD move. Now, we're on a mission to make more BOLD moves as we transform and grow. 

We are proud to provide our community of educators and health-care workers with a bank that puts them first, because they put others first. We're committed to building a unique bank—one that’s different, sustainable, and centred around our Members. 

Join us in breaking the mould and shaping the future of banking. We’re looking for innovative and creative minds that are ready to make a real impact at a place where you can truly be yourself. 
 

So, what’s the role? 

As Senior Manager, Planning & Delivery, you will play an important role in how we plan, deliver and embed change across the organisation. 

You will bring clarity and momentum to our delivery landscape, helping teams align on priorities, manage dependencies and deliver meaningful outcomes. You will also act as an Agile coach, supporting teams to strengthen their ways of working and build capability across the organisation. This is a role for someone who enjoys connecting strategy with delivery and helping teams perform at their best. 
 

In a typical day you will get involved in: 

Leading the planning and delivery of projects across Agile, hybrid and traditional approaches. 

Facilitating cross functional quarterly planning sessions to align teams on priorities, dependencies and resources. 

Leading and supporting a team of project managers and change analysts so they feel empowered and set up for success. 

Coaching teams and leaders on Agile practices that improve collaboration and delivery. 

Helping teams navigate delivery trade-offs, dependencies and planning decisions. 

Overseeing change strategies and communications to support adoption of new systems, processes and ways of working. 

Monitoring delivery performance using clear metrics and insights to support continuous improvement. 

 

About you 

You enjoy bringing people together to solve complex problems and deliver meaningful outcomes. You are comfortable working with senior stakeholders and guiding teams through ambiguity, helping create the clarity that keeps things moving forward. 
 

Ideally you’ll have: 

Senior experience in delivery, program management or portfolio leadership  

Experience working across Agile, hybrid and traditional project environments. 

A background in Agile coaching or delivery leadership, with a strong understanding of Agile principles and practices. 

Experience embedding delivery practices or improving ways of working across teams or organisations. 

A strong track record delivering complex business or technology transformation initiatives. 

Experience leading teams and supporting people to grow and succeed. 

Strong communication and facilitation skills, with the ability to influence and guide senior stakeholders. 

Experience working with tools such as JIRA, MS Project, PPM tools or similar platforms. 
 

Why join us? 

For-purpose, a bank committed to helping its Members and their community. 

Impactful work that will genuinely make a difference to the lives of our Members! 

Work with values-driven leaders, who actively develop talent and support you to grow your career. In an environment that you are empowered to Be Bold. Be Different. Be Sustainable. Be YOU! 

Work-life balance and flexibility, so you can work in a way that best suits you and supports your commitments outside of work. Family friendly parental leave, and paid superannuation up to 52 weeks.  

Hybrid working from our state-of-the-art offices to connect and collaborate, as well as focused time at home. 

Competitive remuneration with an additional one week ‘Be You’ leave, ‘Be Well’ wellbeing benefit, #BFF Club home loan offer and free on-site parking! 

 

To apply 

If you are looking for purpose and an organization that lives its values every day, we encourage you to apply. Likewise, if you tick many but not all the boxes, we also encourage you to apply. Above all else at Bank First, we look for people with heart and learning agility. 

We encourage applications from people of all ages, cultures, abilities, sexual orientation, and gender identities. We strive to provide a recruitment experience that’s fair, inclusive, and accessible.

The position partners closely with asset, markets and investment teams, owning asset-level P&L, balance sheet integrity and forecasting while supporting the onboarding and financial performance of a growing energy asset portfolio. 

Benefits:
Opportunity to work in a high-growth renewable energy and infrastructure environment

Strong commercial partnering across finance, operations and investment teams

Career progression within a rapidly expanding sector
Duties/Key responsibilities:
Own financial performance of the asset portfolio including P&L, cash flow and returns
Lead budgeting, forecasting and integrated financial modelling across asset entities
Maintain balance sheet integrity and oversee month-end close processes
Manage IFRS/AASB technical accounting including lease accounting (IFRS 16)
Provide commercial insights and financial analysis to improve asset performance
Skills and experience:
CA or CPA qualified with a degree in Accounting, Finance or similar
5–8+ years’ experience in energy, infrastructure, utilities or asset-backed businesses
Experience managing asset-level financials including P&L, cash flow and balance sheet
Strong budgeting, forecasting and financial modelling capability
Experience with ERP systems and financial reporting tools
Exposure to data or automation tools such as Power BI or Alteryx highly regarded
Strong understanding of IFRS/AASB accounting standards including lease accounting
Strong stakeholder engagement skills and commercial mindset

Our Technology Team Standards


Balance Speed and Quality

Engineers are expected to balance delivery speed with a strong commitment to quality, meeting agreed timelines while producing reliable, maintainable, and well-tested solutions. Sound judgment in making trade-offs between velocity and long-term sustainability is essential.

Collaborate Effectively

Engineering is collaborative by default. Team members are expected to contribute constructively in design discussions, reviews, and planning, communicate clearly about progress and risks, and support shared team outcomes in both hybrid and distributed environments.

Build and Maintain Systems

Engineers are responsible for building new capabilities while maintaining and improving existing systems. This includes designing scalable solutions, reducing technical debt, supporting operational stability, and contributing to continuous improvement.

Operate with Autonomy

A high degree of autonomy is expected. Given clear objectives, engineers should independently translate problems into actionable technical approaches, proactively identify improvements, and continuously expand relevant technical expertise.

Ownership and Accountability

Ownership is fundamental. Engineers are accountable for the quality, performance, and customer impact of their work from design through post-release support, and are expected to follow through on commitments.

AI-Enabled Engineering

AI is reshaping how software is built, and we are committed to leveraging it as a force multiplier for creativity, impact, and capability. Engineers are expected to confidently apply strong technical fundamentals while embracing AI tools and approaches to enhance productivity, problem-solving, and innovation. Curiosity, adaptability, and enthusiasm for integrating AI into meaningful product development are essential.

Contribute to Team Culture

Engineers contribute positively to a culture of professionalism, transparency, low bureaucracy, and mutual respect, strengthening team performance through authenticity, curiosity, and collaboration.

About the Role


We’re hiring a Data & Analytics Manager who grows high-impact teams that balance efficiency with excellence, value autonomy and creativity, and thrive in a collaborative, inclusive environment.

Team vision: “Enable rapid innovation through a unified data platform”

Lead a high-performing, healthy team

Coach and grow engineers through clear expectations, regular feedback, and thoughtful development plans.
Create an environment where engineers can do focused work, collaborate effectively, and keep improving—without heroics or burnout.
Help build a strong team culture that values autonomy, accountability, and craftsmanship.
Help the team thoughtfully adopt AI-assisted tooling across the software delivery lifecycle to improve developer productivity and code quality.
Lead by example in using AI tools where they add real leverage, and help engineers understand when AI accelerates work—and when human judgment matters most.
Deliver meaningful outcomes in Scrum

Partner with Product and Design to translate goals into a clear plan, then guide the team through execution in a Scrum cadence (planning, delivery, review, retro).
Manage a multi-million dollar product, growing our top and bottom line while improving platform stability
Balance new work, enhancements, bugs, maintenance, and technical debt with sound judgment and transparent trade-offs.
Improve delivery quality and speed over time by reducing friction, improving clarity, and continuously strengthening engineering practices.
Raise the bar for engineering excellence

Champion strong development practices: code reviews that teach, testing discipline, pragmatic documentation, and maintainable implementations.
Encourage “analytical” thinking - creatively solving data problems for our customers is at the heart of this role.
Champion “you build it, you own it” - Deliver product while balancing technical health
Build operational strength (without creating toil)

Help the team build healthy on-call and operational practices, including ownership, runbooks, sensible alerting, and effective incident follow-up.
Lead blameless incident reviews focused on learning and systemic improvements.
Improve observability and system health so the team can confidently ship and support the Core product over time. (This role may include on-call participation in the future.)
Be a strong cross-team partner

Collaborate effectively across Engineering, Product, Design, and Support.
Communicate clearly across time zones and contexts—making decisions understandable and durable.
What Success Looks Like


In this role, success typically means:

Your engineers are growing in skill, confidence, and ownership—and they want to stay on your team.
The team ships consistently with high quality, and reliability improves quarter over quarter.
Technical decisions make the analytics product easier to evolve (not harder).
Cross-functional partners trust your team because expectations, trade-offs, and delivery are clear.
Our Tech Stack!

Data Warehousing: Databricks and Snowflake.
ELT: Fivetran, DLT, DBT
Visualisation: PowerBI Embed
Backend: C# (.NET)
 
About You-- What Sets You Apart!

Previously a Senior+ IC before moving into management (preferably as a data engineer or analytics engineer) .
2+ years experience in Engineering Management.
Proven record leading teams to improve quality and speed through technical + process change.
Depth in distributed systems and data integrity at scale.
Skill planning and delivering sprints with Product and Tech Leads, balancing new work, enhancements, bugs, and maintenance.
Excellent communication across locations/time zones.
Experience in large scale, multi-cloud data warehousing migrations.
Degree in CS or equivalent experience.
Plus if you have accounting domain experience.
Why Work at Karbon?

Gain global experience across the USA, Australia, New Zealand, UK, Canada and the Philippines
4 weeks annual leave plus 5 extra "Karbon Days" off a year
Flexible working environment
Work with (and learn from) an experienced, high-performing team
Be part of a fast-growing company that firmly believes in promoting high performers from within
A collaborative, team-oriented culture that embraces diversity, invests in development, and provides consistent feedback
Generous parental leave

Through the merger of Bank Australia and Qudos Bank we’re creating a better, stronger and more resilient bank for our customers and employees. As one company with two trusted brands, we’re building on the strong foundations of our history and shared values as we bring together the best of both banks.

The Product Advisor – Integration is responsible for managing, coordinating and executing Bank Australia’s product integration activities, with a key focus on delivering the Significant Financial Institution (SFI) uplift program across retail banking products and related processes. The role acts as a bridge between product strategy and execution, ensuring successful delivery of product changes, new developments and project workstreams while maintaining strong adherence to product governance frameworks. The Product Advisor also supports the delivery of broader strategic product integration initiatives as required.

Why join us

We have big plans to become Australia’s most trusted bank and a leading purpose-driven business.
As a certified B Corp, we’re part of a global business community who meet the highest social and environmental standards, putting purpose over profit.
Every role and person in our bank is essential to bringing our values, purpose and aspiration to life.
We offer flexible working options, competitive salary and 14% super.
Our staff have access to a range of meaningful benefits to support their physical and mental wellbeing including 24/7 free counselling, free flu vaccinations and discounted gym memberships.
We also support staff with study assistance, paid parental leave (regardless of gender), volunteer, bereavement, gender affirmation and family violence leave; and the opportunity for bonus annual leave.
We support our staff to grow their careers through learning and development and an organisational culture where we reward and recognise innovation and values, not long hours.
We are committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce that reflects the Australian community.
We care for a conservation reserve – where we work with key partner organisations to protect threatened species and involve staff in conservation.

What you will be doing

Coordinate and lead product requirements for the enterprise‑wide SFI uplift program, ensuring alignment across retail product portfolios.
Manage end‑to‑end delivery of product changes and integration activities, including defining requirements, planning timelines, managing scope and ensuring high‑quality execution.
Liaise with cross‑functional stakeholders to provide updates, KPIs, progress reporting and visibility of product delivery milestones.
Translate prudential, risk and operational SFI requirements into clear product, process and control changes, ensuring regulatory alignment and effective change management.
Support program governance by contributing to evidence gathering, traceability, control uplift and adherence to the Product Governance Framework.
Deliver SFI‑related and integration initiatives on time and to required regulatory and quality standards.
Maintain accurate and complete product documentation, controls and process artefacts.
Build and nurture strong relationships with internal teams (Product, Strategy, PMO, Risk & Compliance, Legal, Customer, Technology, Finance, Lending) and external partners such as consultants and auditors.

What you will bring

Strong ownership of deliverables with the ability to manage dependencies, prioritise effectively and meet deadlines.
Excellent relationship‑building, communication and influencing skills across cross‑functional teams.
Proactive, resilient and agile mindset with a strong alignment to the bank’s values and continuous‑improvement focus.
Ability to interpret prudential and regulatory requirements and translate them into practical product, process and control changes.
Strong analytical, risk‑assessment and problem‑solving capability, providing clear product and delivery advice.
Solid understanding of banking products, product operations, product risk and change delivery within regulated environments.
Familiarity with product systems and data flows across deposits, payments and digital channels.
Strong experience in product management, business analysis, project delivery or related roles within financial services or regulated industries.
Relevant tertiary qualifications in product, business, commerce, project or risk management (desirable).
Proficiency in Microsoft Office and project management tools such as Jira.
Commitment to Bank Australia’s purpose, aspiration, values and brand as well as the B Corp ethos.

By submitting an application you agree to Bank Australia’s Privacy Policy for applicants and confirm that you are legally able to work in Australia. Bank Australia is an equal opportunity employer committed to sustainable development. We encourage people from different backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people from different cultural backgrounds and people with disabilities because we want to reflect the diversity of our communities. We are committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment process and supportive workplace.

Bank Australia reserves the right to withdraw this advertisement at any time. Candidates will be progressed as applications are received, rather than after the advertisement closure date. If you wish to be considered, please submit your application as soon as possible. Please note, the successful candidate will be subject to satisfactory background checks including but not limited to police and previous employment.

Talenza have been engaged by an Australian clean energy company to find an Information Architect to work across a data uplift program of work. The successful candidate will be an experienced data/information architect who has worked with modern microsoft data technologies.

This role requires 3 days per week in the Brisbane office and is only open to candidates currently based in Brisbane with full working rights.



Benefits

Contract opportunity
Competitive day rate up to $1175 + GST
Hybrid working environment (3 days per week in office)
Amazing leadership group and office/team culture
Global ASX listed enterprise
Clean Energy Organisation





Key Requirements

Strong experience designing and governing enterprise data and information architectures that support secure, trusted and accessible data across operational and analytical platforms.
Expertise in cloud data platforms, preferably Microsoft Azure, including services such as Azure Synapse, Data Lake, Data Factory, Azure SQL and Event Hub.
Proven capability in data architecture and engineering, including data modelling, data warehouse/lakehouse design, ETL/ELT pipelines, streaming data and data migration.
Experience designing enterprise integration architectures, including APIs, messaging/service bus patterns and integration between operational systems and analytics platforms.
Strong knowledge of enterprise analytics and reporting platforms, particularly Power BI, including platform architecture, performance monitoring and user adoption.
Experience developing and implementing information architecture and governance, including information classification, taxonomy, ontology and enterprise information asset structuring.

Extraordinary teams building inspiring projects:

Job Title: Principal Consultant - Data and Analytics | Brisbane
Ad Text:
Our Purpose

Here at Datacom, we connect people and technology to solve challenges, create opportunities and discover new possibilities for the communities we live in whilst embodying a culture of trust and respect for employees and customers alike.

Our Team

The Data and Analytics team are a progressive group of diverse and creative individuals with a deep love for all things data related. They build platforms and solutions that are leading edge and at the forefront of the technology curve. A close knit and fun team embedded within our rapidly growing Professional Services division, and part of the wider Datacom ecosystem.

About the Role

As a Principal Consultant, you will lead the technical delivery of large-scale data platform and BI solutions. You will be asked to design, implement and own the delivery of robust Data Platform solutions and end-to-end components. This is a client facing role, so you'll need to be an excellent communicator and good with people. The Principal Consultant works closely with practice leads, project managers, scrum masters, and client stakeholders and teams.

What You’ll Do

Working with clients (businesses users) to gather, clarify requirements and business rules by running workshops with stakeholders.
Lead the technical architecture, design and delivery for a new Data platform.
Provide overall thought leadership to our clients regarding data profiling and integration recommendations.
Data modelling (relational and dimensional like star schema and Snowflake schema)
Managing development life cycle using various source code management tools.
Ability to perform performance tuning and optimisation on new and/or existing data warehouse solutions.
Provide detailed documentation, end user training and knowledge-transfer services to customers, and internal teams.
Mentor developers, acting as an SME in data warehouse architecture and data modelling

What You’ll Bring:

Advanced data modelling skills (Star schema, Snowflake schema, data vault)
Proven track-record of building EDW’s
Advanced ETL /ELT processes design and implementation.
Advanced skills in performance tuning, query plan, query plan analysis, indexing, table partitioning.
Strong skills in cloud platforms such as Azure, AWS & GCP
Strong skills in Azure Data Factory and data Flows  and Azure SQL data platform.
Extensive knowledge of Databricks architecture and features.
Proven track-record  in the design, development and implementation of data solutions using Databricks.
Extensive knowledge of Snowflake’s architecture and features.
Proven track-record  in the design, development and implementation of data solutions using Snowflake.
Experience in data ingestion tools such as Apache Kafka, Informatica, AWS Glue, Azure event hubs, Fivetran, Matillion, DBT and Coalesce.
Experience in Apache Airflow.
Strong consultation and clients’ management skills
Strong understanding of Agile delivery methodology.
Excellent verbal and written communications.

 

Required Skills and Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related discipline
Minimum 8+ years’ experience in EDW design and implementation.
Minimum 8+ years’ experience in ETL architecting and design.
Minimum 8+ years’ experience in data modelling (Star schema & Snowflake schema)
Minimum 5+ years’ experience in semantic layer development.
Minimum 5+ years’ experience in reporting and visualization tools.
Advanced skills in Microsoft BI stack.
Minimum 8+ years’ experience in various Microsoft SQL server technologies including SSIS, SSRS, SSAS
Minimum 4+ years’ experience in design, development and implementation of data solutions using Snowflake.
Minimum 4+ years’ experience in design, development and implementation of data solutions using Databricks.
Wherescape RED, 3D experience is preferred.
Minimum 3+ years’ experience in Power BI.
Advanced skills in Azure, Fabric, and Snowflake pipelines, with experience in tools such as Azure Data Factory, Coalesce, Matillion, dbt, and Fivetran.
Strong skills in data platform implementation, configuration, performance and cost management tuning (i.e. Azure, Fabric, Snowflake).

 

Why join us here at Datacom?

Datacom is one of Australia and New Zealand’s largest suppliers of Information Technology professional services. We have managed to maintain a dynamic, agile, small business feel that is often diluted in larger organisations of our size. It's our people that give Datacom its unique culture and energy that you can feel from the moment you meet with us.

We care about our people and provide a range of perks such as social events, chill-out spaces, remote working, flexi-hours and professional development courses to name a few. You’ll have the opportunity to learn, develop your career, connect and bring your true self to work. You will be recognised and valued for your contributions and be able to do your work in a collegial, flat-structured environment.

We operate at the forefront of technology to help Australia and New Zealand’s largest enterprise organisations explore possibilities and solve their greatest challenges, so you will never run out of interesting new challenges and opportunities.

We want Datacom to be an inclusive and welcoming workplace for everyone and take pride in the steps we have taken and continue to take to make our environment fun and friendly, and our people feel supported.

Job Title: PAYROLL & HR SYSTEMS DATA ANALYST (DAYFORCE) - $120/140k + SUPER - SYD CBD
Ad Text:
Step into a pivotal role on a major payroll and HR systems transformation programme! As a Payroll & HR Systems Data Analyst, you’ll collaborate closely with the project team on the migration of payroll and workforce data from their legacy Preceda platform onto Dayforce, ensuring accuracy, integrity, and seamless delivery.

This is a hands-on role for someone who loves working with complex data, solving problems, and translating numbers into actionable insights. You’ll partner with HR, Payroll, Finance, and project stakeholders, support testing cycles, data migration, documentation mapping, reconcile datasets, and build reports that help drive informed decisions.

WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE:

Use your payroll and HR systems expertise to drive real improvements and shape the future of workforce processes.
Rare opportunity to learn Dayforce or become more proficient with it  
Enjoy a 3 day WFH hybrid model that balances office collaboration with remote flexibility.
Work from a conveniently located city office with easy access to public transport and amenities.
Your contributions are acknowledged through regular recognition initiatives.
Develop your skills while working on a high-profile transformation project in payroll and HR analytics.
Join a collaborative environment that values respect, diversity, and teamwork.
NUTS & BOLTS:
Extract, analyse and validate payroll and workforce data from existing systems
Map and migrate data into a new HRIS platform, including complex or non-standard fields
Support key project phases including system testing, user acceptance testing and parallel payroll runs
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies, defects and reconciliation issues
Develop and maintain clear documentation for data mapping, testing and reconciliation processes
Work collaboratively with HR, Payroll, Finance and external implementation partners
Conduct data quality checks and ensure appropriate data governance standards are maintained
Build HRIS reports and provide stakeholders with useful insights and operational reporting
Identify opportunities to improve reporting capability, data integrity and payroll processes
ABOUT YOU:

Experience analysing payroll or workforce data
Exposure to HRIS, payroll or workforce system implementations or migrations - Dayforce not essential but highly desirable 
Advanced Excel capability including data manipulation, reconciliation and validation
Experience with reporting tools or dashboards is beneficial
Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills
Ability to translate complex data into clear insights for stakeholders
Experience supporting testing cycles within system implementations is advantageous
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Ability to manage competing priorities in a project environment
High level of integrity and ability to handle sensitive information
*For a full JD available upon submitted resume application

Who We Are 
At Torrens University Australia, making a difference is in our DNA. As a Certified B Corporation, we belong to a global community of future-focused companies using business as a force for good. We believe in the transformative power of education to change lives, families, communities – and the world. 

As Australia’s fastest-growing university, we are building modern, scalable digital capabilities that support student success, operational excellence and responsible AI adoption. Our Data Platform is central to this ambition — and we’re now looking for a Principal Data Engineer to help shape its future. 

What’s the Job? 
This is a senior technical leadership role with deep hands-on engineering accountability. 

Reporting to the Head of Data Platform & Governance, you will be the technical authority responsible for designing, building and uplifting Torrens’ cloud-native data platform on Google Cloud Platform (GCP). 

The role will begin with a technical assessment of the current data platform to identify resilience gaps, engineering standards, pipeline reliability improvements and opportunities to introduce scalable platform patterns. 

You’ll step into an environment where foundational standards, DataOps maturity, observability and governance uplift are key priorities. This role will: 

Conduct a technical audit of current pipelines, standards and platform resilience 

Define and implement data engineering design standards, reusable patterns and reference architectures 

Build and optimise modern batch and streaming pipelines 

Establish platform-level CI/CD, IaC and observability frameworks 

Lead technical design reviews for internal and vendor-built pipelines 

Ensure solutions align with enterprise architecture and platform standards 

Define platform SLAs and support model with Operations teams 

Implement monitoring, alerting and incident response frameworks 

Uplift engineering capability while remaining hands-on 

This is not a pure architecture role. We are looking for an engineer-first leader — someone who can design at scale, review code, build pipelines and drive technical quality. 

Key Points About This Role 

Cloud-Native Platform Ownership 
Lead architecture and engineering of scalable GCP data pipelines across batch and streaming environments. 

DataOps & Automation Uplift 
Implement CI/CD, IaC (Terraform), testing and observability to reduce incidents and increase resilience. 

Governance-by-Design Engineering 
Embed metadata, lineage, data quality and privacy controls directly into ingestion and pipeline frameworks. 

Technical Authority & Vendor Oversight 
Provide engineering standards, review vendor outputs and guide cross-functional delivery quality. 

Hands-On Leadership 
Mentor engineers, lead technical reviews and build high-quality, reusable data assets. 

Who You Are 

GCP Engineering Expert 
8+ years in data/cloud engineering with strong hands-on experience across BigQuery, Dataflow, Pub/Sub, Composer/Airflow and Cloud Storage. 

Advanced Programming Capability 
Expert in Python and SQL with experience building scalable, distributed data pipelines. 

Data Platform Observability 
Experience implementing monitoring and observability frameworks for data pipelines, including logging, metrics, alerting and pipeline health tracking. 

DataOps & Automation Leader 
Proven delivery of CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform), observability and automated testing frameworks. 

Data Modelling 
Experience designing analytical data models and optimising schema design for scalable analytical workloads. 

Architecture-Aligned Engineer 
Strong understanding of cloud-native data platform design, streaming architectures and cost optimisation (FinOps). 

Technical Influencer & Mentor 
Confident communicator who can lead code reviews, challenge vendors, uplift standards and operate independently. 

This is a Full-Time, Permanent position based at our Surry Hills Campus in Sydney offering Hybrid working.  

To review the full job description please Click here.  

Why Join Us? 
Bold, modern and agile, we’ve grown from 164 students in 2014 to 40,000+ alumni across 115+ countries. We are building enterprise data capabilities from the ground up — giving you the opportunity to shape standards, define patterns and create a true centre of excellence. 

This role offers architectural influence, hands-on engineering depth and the autonomy to build something meaningful. 

What We Offer 

At Torrens University Australia, you’ll benefit from: 

Study free in a course/degree related to your position Flexible hybrid working arrangements 

B Corp certified university – values driven and purpose led 

Access to internal opportunities to grow and move across the organisation 

A culture that encourages innovation, accountability and impact 

Hiring Process 
We aim to provide you with the information you need at each stage of this process to help you present your best self. If you have any accessibility requirements, please contact your recruiter at Nikeey.silwal@torrens.edu.au for confidential support. 

We are committed to ensuring an equitable, barrier-free application process and have a Reasonable Adjustment Procedure in place to support you throughout the recruitment process. 

We are a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Employer and commit to respond to every applicant. 

To learn more about what makes Torrens University Australia a great place to work, visit https://www.torrens.edu.au/blog. 

We’d love to hear from you.

Identify changes in people, processes, and technology to support effective strategy execution

We are committed to developing our people, offering opportunities for professional and personal growth through structured training, collaboration, exposure to industry leaders, and hands-on learning. 5+ years of work experience in consulting
2+ years leading consulting projects
Tertiary degree
Creative problem solving and strategic thinking skills
Relationship building skills, with the ability to build rapport and maintain strong client connections
Highly developed communication and presentation skills
Adaptable and flexible approach
Strong project delivery experience and commercial mindset

Your new role


JANA is seeking a hands-on data engineering specialist to own and evolve the enterprise data warehouse, built in a Microsoft Fabric environment using a medallion architecture from bronze through gold.


The role is responsible for transforming complex, investment-grade data into trusted, analytics-ready datasets that underpin research, portfolio analysis, reporting, and AI-driven insights.


This position sits at the crossroads of analytics engineering, data modelling & third-party data management, and requires deep domain expertise in funds management and wealth management. It is a senior individual contributor role with real ownership across data quality, semantics, and delivery.


This role is located in our Melbourne office and reports to the GM, Technology and Data.

Job Title: Senior Advisor - Mine Economics & Financial Modelling
Ad Text:
A transition opportunity for a Private Equity & Investment Banking professional

Looking to apply your investment skillset to real assets and long-term capital decisions?



Mining is one of the few industries where geology, engineering and economics intersect to determine asset value, making it one of the most intellectually interesting sectors for investment professionals.



You will work alongside mining engineers, geologists, decarbonisation and gas management specialists and operational experts to understand how geology, mine planning, processing, cost structures and carbon reduction initiatives drive asset value across critical minerals, bulk commodities and precious metals.



Palaris is seeking a Senior Advisor - Mine Economics and Financial Modelling to join our advisory team in Newcastle or Brisbane, working at the intersection of investment analysis and mining operations. This is a hands-on analytical role focused on economic modelling, valuation and investment analysis of mining assets.



If you enjoy complex investment analysis and are wanting to broaden your skill base and build an important step for future career progression, this is the role that will do that.



About Us



Palaris is a specialist advisory firm to the mining and resources sector across the world. With over 5,000 projects delivered in over 50 countries across 30 different commodity types, we are the trusted advisor to resource companies, financial institutions, equity investors, and government agencies.



What You Will Gain



1. Work with real, high value assets

Work on mining projects involves billions in capital and decades-long horizons.

Get exposure to asset-heavy activities rarely seen in traditional PE/IB environments.

2. Stay transaction-focused - with more balance

Work on buy-side/sell-side due diligence, valuations, economic modelling and scenario planning.

Keep the analytical challenge of PE/IB, without the lifestyle trade-off (work a 9-day fortnight).

3. Develop technical insight that investors value

Learn how geology, mine planning, cost curves and processing constraints shape asset value - working directly with mining engineers, geologists and operational specialists.

Build expertise that opens doors to natural resources investing, corporate development or strategic roles inside major miners.

About the Role

Lead economic and financial modelling across full asset lifecycles

Build and interrogate economic models integrating geology, mine planning, processing and cost assumptions

Assess project economics, commodity sensitivities and capital structures

Integrate technical inputs into valuations and investment cases

Support investment and transaction processes from early screening through to negotiation and decision

Prepare clear, investment-ready reports and recommendations

Contribute to investment decision frameworks and technical due diligence for lenders, investors and mining companies

About You

Degree in Finance/Economics or Commerce

Solid experience from PE, IB or Transaction Services

Advanced financial modelling skills and excel expertise

Excellent reporting writing skills and presentation skills

Demonstrated analytical ability to build and interrogate economic models



No mining experience is required, we will immerse you in the world of mining, working alongside mining professionals to understand the technical drivers that shape asset value. Your investment perspective will add depth to our multidisciplinary team.



Interested?



If you are someone who often says “why not” – let’s have a conversation, call Chris Anderson, HR Manager on (02) 4911 2218 or apply now and we’ll be in touch.



Applicants must have full work rights for Australia, unfortunately, we are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this position.

Experience in the health services sector—particularly with medical, clinical, and patient data—is essential. Strong capability in BI/data/reporting environments is also required. Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal, will be critical to success.

Your day at NTT

Work with business stakeholders to document health data reporting and data collection processes.
Analyse existing documentation and identify gaps, inconsistencies, and improvement opportunities.
Develop operational and technical business process documentation for both internal and external stakeholders.
Produce end‑to‑end process flow diagrams, including data flow artefacts across multiple systems.
Create integration technical specifications covering interfaces, security, non‑functional requirements, and system dependencies.
Compile supporting documentation such as FAQs, user access guides, tutorials, and dataset reference materials.

What would make you a good fit for this role?

Demonstrated experience working with health sector data (clinical, medical, patient records, reporting systems).
Strong background in BI/data/reporting environments and related documentation.
Proven ability to engage, negotiate, and collaborate with business stakeholders, technical teams, and product owners.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, structured documentation.
Experience working within government environments and navigating complex stakeholder groups.
Ability to lead workshops, capture business processes, and translate findings into high‑quality artefacts.

Deliver high-quality program assurance reviews across a dynamic portfolio of work.
Be part of a new Centre of Excellence, shaping how program assurance is delivered.
Join a strong global Internal Audit team known for quality, consistency, and trusted partnership.

Our people are customer obsessed. They prioritise the needs and satisfaction of the customer above all else. ?Our mindset fosters innovation and creates strong, lasting customer relationships as we strive to be the most customer centric company in Australia and New Zealand. 

In the role of Audit Manager, you’ll join a newly established Centre of Excellence Program Assurance team that’s focused on keeping it simple, working collaboratively with the business, and delivering assurance that genuinely adds value.

Each day you will go above and beyond to:

Lead the planning, execution, and reporting of program assurance and audit engagements in line with the Audit Charter and Annual Audit Plan.
Lead end-to-end audits from planning through to reporting, providing clear insight on risk, control effectiveness, and delivery assurance for major programs.
Identify key risks and assess the design and operating effectiveness of controls, supported by high-quality workpapers.
Project manage an audit or concurrent audits, delivering to agreed timeframes and resourcing.

Proactively manage risk, compliance, and policy obligations, escalating issues as required.

What you’ll bring

Demonstrated experience in a Senior Analyst or Manager-level Internal Audit role, with a strong understanding of risk and control frameworks.
Program delivery or project assurance experience (highly desirable).

Excellent organisational skills and attention to detail.

Prior experience auditing within Financial Services (advantageous).

Contribute to a premier economics department and shape future economic leaders.


Apply now to join a globally recognised economics department and drive cutting-edge research in econometrics.


Investing in you - benefits package including salary packaging, health and wellbeing programs, discounted services, and professional development opportunities.

The University of Melbourne
We take pride in our people, who all contribute to our mission to benefit society through the transformative impact of education and research. Discover more via our website.

Your next career opportunity
As a Senior Lecturer or Associate Professor in Econometrics at the University of Melbourne, you'll join one of Australia's premier economics departments. You'll have the opportunity to conduct influential research, teach at the forefront of econometrics, and shape the next generation of economic thinkers. Your expertise will contribute to our department's global reputation and impact economic policy and practice.

Your responsibilities will include:

Conduct High-Impact Research: Publish in top-tier journals, secure competitive grants, and engage with international academic networks to advance econometric knowledge.


Deliver Excellence in Teaching: Develop and teach specialist courses in econometrics at undergraduate and postgraduate levels, fostering student success and satisfaction.


Provide Academic Leadership: Mentor junior colleagues, contribute to departmental initiatives, and represent the university in professional forums.


Supervise Research Students: Guide Honours, Masters, and PhD students, creating a rigorous and supportive research environment.

You may be a great fit if:
You are an accomplished econometrician with a strong track record of publications in leading journals, a passion for teaching excellence, and the ability to secure competitive research funding and contribute to the department's international standing

You may also:

Have a PhD in Economics or Econometrics with a focus on advanced quantitative methods.


Possess a robust publication record in top-tier economics and econometrics journals.


Demonstrate experience in securing competitive research grants and supervising postgraduate students.


Have proven teaching excellence at undergraduate and postgraduate levels in econometrics and quantitative methods.


Show a commitment to academic leadership and the ability to contribute to the department's strategic goals.

For further information please refer to the attached PD.

What we offer you!
We offer the opportunity to be part of a vibrant community and enjoy a comprehensive range of benefits to support your success and sense of fulfillment, including:

Supportive flexible work arrangements underpinned by our commitment to inclusion and well-being


Progressive, considerate leave provisions to empower your work-life balance


Salary packaging and access to a range of discounted services including Bupa health insurance


Health and well-being services including a leading Employee Assistance Program

For more information check out our benefits page!

Your new team - Department of Economics
The Department of Economics at the University of Melbourne is a leading economics department in Australia. It has 95 academic staff and is part of a larger community of economists at the university. This includes the Melbourne Business School with 11 academic economists and econometricians and the Melbourne Institute of Applied Economic and Social Research with 43 researchers undertaking research and consultancy projects for government and private sector clients.

The Faculty of Business and Economics holds Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) accreditation and European Quality Improvement System (EQUIS) accreditation. It hosts world class academics and professional staff, the brightest students, alumni, and other stakeholders. We are committed to enhancing our applied research outcomes, expanding our curriculum, and bolstering our connections with industry to help shape the world of business and economics in Australia and internationally.

Struber is a national, human‑based services consultancy. About the Role

As a Senior Consultant, you will play a hands‑on role in delivering people‑centered work while helping shape how we grow in Victoria.




You will:




Deliver and lead meaningful work

Lead and deliver people‑focused projects across engagement, communication and change

Work confidently in complex, fast‑moving or start‑up environments

Support clients through sensitive, high‑impact issues affecting communities, customers, staff or partners

Bring structure, rigour and momentum to ambiguous problems

Drive clarity and change

Design and implement communication, engagement and change approaches

Develop messaging, narratives, content, submissions, bids and project materials

Translate technical, policy or program information into clear, accessible and human‑centered outputs

Apply sound planning, risk management and delivery discipline

Build trusted relationships

Partner closely with clients and stakeholders, from community members to senior executives

Facilitate conversations, manage issues, and build shared understanding and alignment

Represent Struber with confidence and professionalism in client environments

Contribute to culture and capability

Coach and support consultants and emerging talent

Role‑model Struber’s Bright Mantras: pace, clarity, energy and commercial discipline

Actively contribute to a positive, inclusive and high‑performance culture

Help shape how we work as the Victorian practice grows




About You




You’ll thrive at Struber if you:

Are comfortable working in ambiguity and change

Bring a start‑up mindset – adaptable, proactive and solutions‑focused

Care deeply about culture, people and impact

Balance human‑centered thinking with commercial awareness and delivery discipline

Change management, behaviour change or social impact

Human‑centered design or service design

Bids, tenders, proposals or submissions

Project management in people‑focused environments




You might come from:

Social impact or community‑focused organisations

Project manage high-impact Saas implementations for Government clients.

Drive the roadmap of upgrades and releases.

Hybrid working, supportive team and a pipeline of interesting projects.


About the Role


Insight Data Solutions is a business within a business - using technology to drive efficiency and transparency around land valuations, leveraging new digital capabilities that will minimise bottlenecks and create process efficiencies in delivering valuations for Government entities e.g. council owned properties and land.

Through our unique mix of robust technology and comprehensive industry expertise, IDS enables governments, corporates and consumers to meet their property valuation needs.


As a Project Manager, you'll play a key role in the improvement of Government services through innovation, as we create the national property platform for Government. Working with Product & Engineering teams, you'll drive the successful implementation of VM Online - our Computer Assisted Mass Appraisal (CAMA) software, to Victorian Government from planning to delivery, and support an ongoing roadmap of upgrades and releases with the help of project and support teams.


12 month contract.

Put simply, our Data Solutions division is a true market leader! This is a unique opportunity to join a unique "business within a business", in a very supportive and close-knit culture with a huge pipeline of interesting and varied projects on the horizon to look forward to.


In a typical day you can expect to:


Lead end-to-end delivery of our SaaS implementation projects for government clients - owning scope, timelines, budget and quality while keeping everyone aligned and informed.

Partner closely with Product, Engineering and PMO teams to deliver high-impact outcomes using our hybrid Agile and Waterfall delivery practices.

Act as a trusted client-facing advisor - building strong stakeholder relationships and ensuring each project delivers real business value and ROI.

Drive governance and reporting frameworks that give clarity and confidence to our PMO and Executive teams.

Identify, manage and mitigate risks, issues and change requests - keeping projects moving smoothly and transparently.

Track project performance, financials and success metrics - documenting milestones and ensuring benefits are realised in line with the business case.

Plan and coordinate release activities - managing cross-team dependencies, resolving challenges and communicating release impacts clearly.

Contribute to a high-performing, collaborative team culture - embedding best practice, encouraging engagement and helping shape the future of government property technology.


Why Join Us:


We're the right-sized business for you to make a real impact, with a workplace culture where you can be you. There's so many perks! Here's some of the favourites…


Hybrid working between home, office and visiting clients.
Wellbeing Leave.
Leadership development programs, LinkedIn Learning and more.

Regular social events including our famous Innovation Days and annual BBQ!



Our Ideal Person:


ESSENTIAL:


A Bachelors degree or equivalent from an accredited university or college.

Proficient in Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC).

Experience delivering to Government or large corporate clients.

Experiencing working closely with Product and Technical teams.

Experience with Waterfall, Agile and Lean methodologies.

Superior communication and relationship management skills.

Highly productive, with the ability to lead and work both independently and as a team member.


NICE TO HAVES:


Agile Scrum or similar Project Management certification (preferred).
